question: 为什么预测拉塞尔第一？
answer:
这里要先区分两个口径：George Russell 是按平均完赛名次排第一，不是按冠军概率排第一。当前预测里 George Russell 的平均完赛名次是 2.718，预计排名为第 1；冠军概率第一的是 Kimi Antonelli（45.33%）。

George Russell 能在平均完赛名次上排到第一，主要因为模型给了他很高的领奖台概率 (81.17%) 和积分区概率 (97.50%)，坏结果尾部比其他争冠车手略低。

排位输入方面，George Russell 的同场排位名次是 第 4，这个输入主要影响发车顺序和起步后的赛道位置。

我不会再把 George Russell 的内部能力分当作解释证据来展示。这个分数里混有车队和赛车基础强度先验、车手基础能力先验、正赛攻防和比赛执行先验、赛车与赛道类型适配、车队策略能力、湿地能力按天气概率折算、保胎能力等静态 seed 先验；这些先验来自本地种子数据，不是由本场新闻、排位、练习赛、近期分站结果或车队技术信息直接计算出来的事实。除非这些先验被重新标定并绑定来源，否则它们只能作为模型风险提示，不能作为“为什么预测如此”的证据。

最直接支撑这个判断的输入包括：车手 George Russell 的排位速度输入，依据是：来源：同场 FastF1 排位结果；同场排位名次为 第 4/22，作为排位和发车位信号，不直接当作正赛速度，方向为正向；车队 Mercedes 的正赛速度输入，依据是：来源：FastF1 本赛季车队强度重估；截止本场前车队每站积分对比为 33.50，全场平均 9.18，作为赛车/车队强度重估，方向为正向；车队 Mercedes 的排位速度输入，依据是：来源：同场 FastF1 排位结果；同场车队平均排位名次为 2.50，作为车队排位状态输入，方向为正向；车队 Mercedes 的正赛速度输入，依据是：来源：F1 官方积分榜；官方车队积分榜信息为 第 1，302.0 分，全场平均 83.3，作为车队状态先验，方向为正向。

注意：这次预测的状态是诊断专用，阻塞项为：Codex 证据质量仍需复核、概率校准仍停留在诊断级。因此这是一份模型机制解释，不是稳定盈利优势证明。
limitations: 当前预测包状态不是正式可用于盈利优势判断，只能用于诊断解释。
正式使用前需要解决阻塞项：Codex 证据质量仍需复核。
正式使用前需要解决阻塞项：概率校准仍停留在诊断级。
部分 Codex 证据仍然偏弱或需要复核，解释中相关判断不能当作强事实。
当前蒙特卡洛采样次数较低，小概率事件和 0% 概率可能受采样分辨率影响。

features.explanation: Same-event FastF1 qualifying classification before British Grand Prix: P4/22; used as a strong cutoff-valid qualifying/grid signal. It primarily affects sampled grid order and is not treated as direct race pace.

Cutoff-valid FastF1 race results before British Grand Prix: team total points per race 33.50 vs field 9.18; recent window 27.67 vs field 9.18. Used as a bounded team/car strength reestimate so current-season results can move the race-pace prior without hand-writing a team order.
Same-event FastF1 qualifying team average position before British Grand Prix: 2.50; used as a team-level qualifying form signal.
Official constructor standings before British Grand Prix: P1, 302.0 points vs field average 83.3; used as a cutoff-safe team form prior.
Practice 1 long-run proxy before British Grand Prix: 93.134s vs field 94.350s over 9 clean lap(s); used as a same-weekend race-pace signal.
Qualifying best valid lap before British Grand Prix: 88.481s vs field 89.500s; used as gap-aware qualifying pace, separate from the qualifying classification/grid-order feature.
Official driver standings before British Grand Prix: P2, 131.0 points vs field average 41.6; used as a cutoff-safe season form prior, not a manual team-strength override.
Previous 3 race grid result(s) before British Grand Prix: average grid 2.67 vs field 11.50; used as point-in-time qualifying form prior.
Previous 3 race result(s) before British Grand Prix: average points 14.33 vs field 4.59; used as point-in-time race form prior.
Official constructor standings before British Grand Prix: P1, 302.0 points; converted into a smaller qualifying prior because standings primarily reflect race outcomes.
Qualifying team best-lap proxy before British Grand Prix: 88.296s vs team field 89.500s; used as same-weekend car qualifying pace.
Historical OpenF1 analogue rank 1/10 in Qualifying_9554; used as low-confidence qualifying_pace prior.
Season-to-date FastF1 grid results before British Grand Prix: average grid 2.38 vs field 11.50; used as a broader qualifying-form prior.
Team average driver points over previous 3 race(s): 13.83 vs field 4.59; used as point-in-time team form prior.
Season-to-date FastF1 race results before British Grand Prix: 8 race(s), driver average points 13.75 vs field 4.59; used as a broader form prior than the recent-window signal.
Team average driver points across 8 race(s) before British Grand Prix: 16.75 vs field 4.59; used as a season-to-date team form prior.
Historical OpenF1 analogue rank 8/10 in Practice_2_9552; used as low-confidence race_pace prior.
Historical OpenF1 analogue rank 3/10 in Practice_3_9553; used as low-confidence race_pace prior.
Official driver standings before British Grand Prix: P2, 131.0 points; converted into a smaller qualifying prior because standings primarily reflect race outcomes.
Practice 1 team long-run proxy before British Grand Prix: 93.134s vs team field 94.257s; used as same-weekend car race pace.
Historical OpenF1 analogue rank 4/10 in Practice_1_9551; used as low-confidence race_pace prior.
Practice 1 speed-trap average before British Grand Prix: 288.4 kph vs field 276.9 kph; used as a small straight-line signal.
FastF1 recent-vs-older team form before British Grand Prix: relative points delta -4.67; used as team momentum signal.
1 non-finished classification(s) across 8 cutoff-valid FastF1 result(s); used as a season reliability risk prior.
Historical analogue race had meaningful rainfall; driver wet-skill prior gets a small confidence-weighted boost.
Practice 1 tyre-degradation proxy before British Grand Prix: +0.0229s/lap vs field -0.2481s/lap; lower degradation improves strategy pace.
Previous 3 finished race result(s) before British Grand Prix: average opportunity-normalized grid-to-finish conversion -0.14 vs field +0.28; used as point-in-time race execution prior.
FastF1 recent-vs-older form before British Grand Prix: relative points delta 0.93; used as momentum signal so late-season improvement or decline can move the race-pace prior.
Team opportunity-normalized finished-race grid-to-finish conversion over previous 3 race(s): -0.02 vs field +0.32; used as point-in-time team race execution prior.
FastF1 recent-vs-older grid form before British Grand Prix: relative grid delta -0.47; used as qualifying momentum signal.
Season-to-date FastF1 finished races before British Grand Prix: average opportunity-normalized grid-to-finish conversion -0.04 vs field +0.24; used as a broader race execution prior.
FastF1 recent-vs-older finished-race grid conversion before British Grand Prix: relative conversion delta -0.29; used as race execution momentum.
FastF1 recent-vs-older team grid conversion before British Grand Prix: relative conversion delta -0.28; used as team race execution momentum.
Team average opportunity-normalized finished-race grid-to-finish conversion across 8 race(s) before British Grand Prix: +0.08 vs field +0.26; used as a season-to-date team race execution prior.

evidence_context.question: 为什么预测拉塞尔第一？

features.explanation: Same-event FastF1 qualifying classification before British Grand Prix: P4/22; used as a strong cutoff-valid qualifying/grid signal. It primarily affects sampled grid order and is not treated as direct race pace.

Cutoff-valid FastF1 race results before British Grand Prix: team total points per race 33.50 vs field 9.18; recent window 27.67 vs field 9.18. Used as a bounded team/car strength reestimate so current-season results can move the race-pace prior without hand-writing a team order.
Same-event FastF1 qualifying team average position before British Grand Prix: 2.50; used as a team-level qualifying form signal.
Official constructor standings before British Grand Prix: P1, 302.0 points vs field average 83.3; used as a cutoff-safe team form prior.
Practice 1 long-run proxy before British Grand Prix: 93.134s vs field 94.350s over 9 clean lap(s); used as a same-weekend race-pace signal.
Qualifying best valid lap before British Grand Prix: 88.481s vs field 89.500s; used as gap-aware qualifying pace, separate from the qualifying classification/grid-order feature.
Official driver standings before British Grand Prix: P2, 131.0 points vs field average 41.6; used as a cutoff-safe season form prior, not a manual team-strength override.
Previous 3 race grid result(s) before British Grand Prix: average grid 2.67 vs field 11.50; used as point-in-time qualifying form prior.
Previous 3 race result(s) before British Grand Prix: average points 14.33 vs field 4.59; used as point-in-time race form prior.
Official constructor standings before British Grand Prix: P1, 302.0 points; converted into a smaller qualifying prior because standings primarily reflect race outcomes.
Qualifying team best-lap proxy before British Grand Prix: 88.296s vs team field 89.500s; used as same-weekend car qualifying pace.
Historical OpenF1 analogue rank 1/10 in Qualifying_9554; used as low-confidence qualifying_pace prior.
Season-to-date FastF1 grid results before British Grand Prix: average grid 2.38 vs field 11.50; used as a broader qualifying-form prior.
Team average driver points over previous 3 race(s): 13.83 vs field 4.59; used as point-in-time team form prior.
Season-to-date FastF1 race results before British Grand Prix: 8 race(s), driver average points 13.75 vs field 4.59; used as a broader form prior than the recent-window signal.
Team average driver points across 8 race(s) before British Grand Prix: 16.75 vs field 4.59; used as a season-to-date team form prior.
Historical OpenF1 analogue rank 8/10 in Practice_2_9552; used as low-confidence race_pace prior.
Historical OpenF1 analogue rank 3/10 in Practice_3_9553; used as low-confidence race_pace prior.
Official driver standings before British Grand Prix: P2, 131.0 points; converted into a smaller qualifying prior because standings primarily reflect race outcomes.
Practice 1 team long-run proxy before British Grand Prix: 93.134s vs team field 94.257s; used as same-weekend car race pace.
Historical OpenF1 analogue rank 4/10 in Practice_1_9551; used as low-confidence race_pace prior.
Practice 1 speed-trap average before British Grand Prix: 288.4 kph vs field 276.9 kph; used as a small straight-line signal.
FastF1 recent-vs-older team form before British Grand Prix: relative points delta -4.67; used as team momentum signal.
1 non-finished classification(s) across 8 cutoff-valid FastF1 result(s); used as a season reliability risk prior.
Historical analogue race had meaningful rainfall; driver wet-skill prior gets a small confidence-weighted boost.
Practice 1 tyre-degradation proxy before British Grand Prix: +0.0229s/lap vs field -0.2481s/lap; lower degradation improves strategy pace.
Previous 3 finished race result(s) before British Grand Prix: average opportunity-normalized grid-to-finish conversion -0.14 vs field +0.28; used as point-in-time race execution prior.
FastF1 recent-vs-older form before British Grand Prix: relative points delta 0.93; used as momentum signal so late-season improvement or decline can move the race-pace prior.
Team opportunity-normalized finished-race grid-to-finish conversion over previous 3 race(s): -0.02 vs field +0.32; used as point-in-time team race execution prior.
FastF1 recent-vs-older grid form before British Grand Prix: relative grid delta -0.47; used as qualifying momentum signal.
Season-to-date FastF1 finished races before British Grand Prix: average opportunity-normalized grid-to-finish conversion -0.04 vs field +0.24; used as a broader race execution prior.
FastF1 recent-vs-older finished-race grid conversion before British Grand Prix: relative conversion delta -0.29; used as race execution momentum.
FastF1 recent-vs-older team grid conversion before British Grand Prix: relative conversion delta -0.28; used as team race execution momentum.
Team average opportunity-normalized finished-race grid-to-finish conversion across 8 race(s) before British Grand Prix: +0.08 vs field +0.26; used as a season-to-date team race execution prior.

top_features.explanation: Same-event FastF1 qualifying classification before British Grand Prix: P4/22; used as a strong cutoff-valid qualifying/grid signal. It primarily affects sampled grid order and is not treated as direct race pace.

Cutoff-valid FastF1 race results before British Grand Prix: team total points per race 33.50 vs field 9.18; recent window 27.67 vs field 9.18. Used as a bounded team/car strength reestimate so current-season results can move the race-pace prior without hand-writing a team order.
Same-event FastF1 qualifying team average position before British Grand Prix: 2.50; used as a team-level qualifying form signal.
Official constructor standings before British Grand Prix: P1, 302.0 points vs field average 83.3; used as a cutoff-safe team form prior.
Practice 1 long-run proxy before British Grand Prix: 93.134s vs field 94.350s over 9 clean lap(s); used as a same-weekend race-pace signal.
